title: CLI lab build — physical layer (Phase 1)
description: Answer these before writing lab HTML or LAB CONFIG. Later phases (L2/L3 tasks, grading) build on this spec.

questions.prompt: Which track is this lab for?
Display title for the lab page
How many network devices (routers + switches + WLC/AP) are in the topology?
List each device: hostname, role, and platform type
How many end hosts (PC / server) need a CLI or ping prompt?
Default number of active router interfaces to configure?
questions.help: Routers use Ethernet0/0–0/3 first, then Serial0/0/0–0/1, etc.

questions.prompt: Default number of active switch interfaces to configure?
questions.help: Switches use Ethernet0/0 upward (E0/0, E0/1, …) for each active count.

questions.prompt: If any device needs a different active interface count, note hostname and count
List each link: device A + interface → device B + interface
If reusing, path under public/ (e.g. CCNA-Study/CCNA_labs/images/foo.png)

sections.title: Interface admin / line status at lab start

questions.prompt: For each used interface, initial status at lab open

stateFields.notes: Why (optional: err-disabled, shutdown, cable unplugged sim)

questions.help: Typical working link: admin up, protocol up. Break scenarios may use administratively down down.

questions.prompt: Any speed/duplex details for the scenario? (auto, 100/full, mismatch, etc.)
Simulated physical faults to fix in the lab? (none, bad cable, wrong cable type, shutdown port, …)

agentInstructions: Do not create or edit lab HTML until physical-layer answers are collected (unless the user explicitly says to skip).
Use AskQuestion for choice fields when possible; use chat for device_list, link_list, and interface_state_list.
After answers are confirmed, write data/lab-builds/{slug}-physical.json and summarize the topology before Phase 2 (L2/L3 tasks).
When implementing, copy templates/lab-build/cli-lab-realistic.html and map devices/links into LAB CONFIG and show running-config snapshots.
